reviews.review_description: I'm a 62 man who has seen all kinds of horror films and experienced quite a lot in life for real and as silly as I know I am and know I'm in no real danger, this game, especially the full game is utterly terrifying!!!🤣
If you don't have a gear vr it won't work!!XD  But the game is amazing 5 stars!!

Great game, but I can't seem to get to far before my phone is overheating...very unfortunate, because this is a grade A creepy well done game. If not for the overheating, it would be an easy 5 stars.
OMG I love this game and it was so scary love it!!
Or does your screen spin constantly while connected to a controller

A horror VR game is the best. I love a good scare. I spent like 2 hours debating on buying the game. I eventually said no because i didnt know if i would be wasting money. Thanks to you brilliant devs for releasing this demo, i have bought the full game. This is my favorite VR Game. And yes it requires a controller, read the description. If your bluetooth controller does not work, resync it to your phone by holding X and the home button. That syncs it into gamepad mode rather than keyboard mode.
still ok in 2018
Please can you make this game VR controller compatible
Great app for relaxing and meditating about you impending death.
Using Gear VR and only played the free demo but was impressed with what I saw.

Dreadhalls is very immersive. It puts you right into the action. Sound effects really set it off. You will experience temporary nausea/dizziness with VR gear, however if you want to enjoy/experience VR entertainment, it will pass. DREADHALLS put you face to face with horror.  For a great,  thrilling VR experience DREADHALLS should be your first purchase!

I played this on the Oculus GO. This is a good demo, and a decent length, which bodes well for the finished game being long, as so many VR games are too short. I liked the game-play and the creatures that stalk you are actually quite scary, although sometimes a little too hard to avoid. Anyway, there were a couple of things I would have liked to be different.

1. There is no 'reverse' i.e. you can't walk backwards. This is needed for when you have walked too close to a door and need to back up a bit to use your inventory.
2. The inventory is tricky to use and impossible when too close to something, such as a door. It could do without the effect that it gets closer to you as you approach a vertical surface as it pushes it so close you can't really use it. A 'laser pointer' would also be useful.
3. The demo doesn't save you progress, I hope the finished game will, but even then the distance you go back in the game when you die is a bit excessive. Perhaps either put in restore points or have at least a 2 hit health bar, as those monsters do sometimes come out of nowhere and you're just dead.

Otherwise it's great.
I have found a Bluetooth controller it could be good
